records.ety: [OE. aqueinten, acointen, OF. acointier, LL. adcognitare, fr. L. ad + cognitus, p. p. of cognoscere to know; con- + noscere to know. See Quaint, Know.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Acquainted; p. pr. & vb. n. Acquainting.]

defs: To furnish or give experimental knowledge of; to make (one) to know; to make familiar; -- followed by with.
quotes: Before a man can speak on any subject, it is necessary to be acquainted with it.
A man of sorrows and acquainted with grief.

defs: To communicate notice to; to inform; to make cognizant; -- followed by with (formerly, also, by of), or by that, introducing the intelligence; as, to acquaint a friend with the particulars of an act.
quotes: Acquaint her here of my son Paris' love.
I must acquaint you that I have received New dated letters from Northumberland.
